data.content: I can see the tombstone has .NET runtime frames from three libraries. Let me try to download debug symbols from Microsoft's symbol server and symbolicate them.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line dotnet-symbol 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'binutils|llvm' | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading libmonosgen-2.0.so symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
mkdir -p /tmp/symbols

# Try several URL formats for the symbol server
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILE="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Format 1: elf-buildid-sym-<id>
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILE}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILE}"
echo " <- elf-buildid-sym format"

# Format 2: just the buildid
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILE}/${BUILDID}/${FILE}"
echo " <- plain buildid format"

# Format 3: _.debug file
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
echo " <- _.debug format"

# Format 4: libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILE}.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILE}.dbg"
echo " <- .dbg format"
arguments.description: Try downloading mono symbols from MS symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Try downloading mono symbols from MS symbol server

data.reasoningText:
Now I need to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames. The tombstone has several libraries:
1. `libmonosgen-2.0.so` (BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be) - frames #01, #02, #03, #06
2. `libSystem.Native.so` (BuildId: 7a3b5c9d1e4f6082a3c5d7e9f1b2d4a6c8e0f213) - frames #04, #07


3. `libSystem.Globalization.Native.so` (BuildId: 2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e) - frame #05

These are .NET runtime native libraries, and I'll need to locate the debug symbols matching these specific BuildIds. Microsoft publishes symbol packages for this, so I can use the `dotnet-symbol` tool or query the Microsoft symbol server directly at `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download`.

For ELF binaries, I need to construct the right URL format using the BuildId as the key—typically something like `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/<filename>/<buildid_lowercase>/_.debug`. Let me reconsider the exact convention for symbolicating these Android tombstones with the Microsoft symbol server.

I can use `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` with the debug symbols once I download them. The BuildIds I'm working with are 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be for libmonosgen-2.0.so and 7a3b5c9d for libSystem.Native.so, so I'll attempt to fetch the symbols using those identifiers.

For the Microsoft symbol server, I'm trying to figure out the correct URL pattern for ELF files—the SymStore convention appears to be `<server>/filename/elf-buildid-sym-full/filename`, though I should verify this against the .NET diagnostics documentation. I could also try using `dotnet-symbol` directly if it's available, or make HTTP requests to the symbol server myself. I'm trying to pin down the exact URL format for fetching ELF debug symbols from Microsoft's symbol server, but I'm not entirely certain of the pattern. Let me just try a few different approaches—checking what tools are available locally, attempting downloads from the symbol server, and using whatever symbolication tool I can find to work with the debug information.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line dotnet-symbol 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'binutils|llvm' | head -5

# Try downloading libmonosgen-2.0.so symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
mkdir -p /tmp/symbols

# Try several URL formats for the symbol server
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILE="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Format 1: elf-buildid-sym-<id>
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILE}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILE}"
echo " <- elf-buildid-sym format"

# Format 2: just the buildid
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILE}/${BUILDID}/${FILE}"
echo " <- plain buildid format"

# Format 3: _.debug file
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
echo " <- _.debug format"

# Format 4: libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILE}.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILE}.dbg"
echo " <- .dbg format"

toolArgs.command:
# Try downloading libmonosgen-2.0.so symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
mkdir -p /tmp/symbols

# Try several URL formats for the symbol server
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILE="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Format 1: elf-buildid-sym-<id>
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILE}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILE}"
echo " <- elf-buildid-sym format"

# Format 2: just the buildid
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILE}/${BUILDID}/${FILE}"
echo " <- plain buildid format"

# Format 3: _.debug file
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
echo " <- _.debug format"

# Format 4: libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILE}.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILE}.dbg"
echo " <- .dbg format"

  0x0000000000222098 \
  0x0000000000222234 \
  0x000000000019bac0 \
  0x000000000019bc14

data.reasoningText:
The debug file is valid and matches the BuildId from the tombstone. Now I need to symbolicate the frames. Since this is an ARM64 binary and I'm on x86_64, I need to use the aarch64 version of addr2line. I see `binutils-aarch64-linux-gnu` is installed, so `aarch64-linux-gnu-addr2line` should be available.

The frames from libmonosgen-2.0.so are:
- #01 pc 0000000000222098
- #02 pc 0000000000222234
- #03 pc 000000000019bac0


- #06 pc 000000000019bc14

I've also got frames from libSystem.Native.so and libSystem.Globalization.Native.so, but I only have symbols for the monosgen library, so I'll focus on symbolicating those frames first.

arguments.command:
# Also try to get symbols for libSystem.Native.so and libSystem.Globalization.Native.so
# Try dotnet-symbol with buildid approach, or alternate URL patterns

# Try the library itself (not _.debug) for System.Native
BUILDID="7a3b5c9d1e4f6082a3c5d7e9f1b2d4a6c8e0f213"
FILE="libSystem.Native.so"
for fmt in "elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}" "elf-buildid-${BUILDID}" "${BUILDID}"; do
  code=$(curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILE}/${fmt}/${FILE}")
  echo "${code} <- ${FILE} / ${fmt}"
done

# Try System.Globalization.Native

  echo "${code} <- ${FILE} / ${fmt}"
done
